markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: TLN Pirates vs Esprit Shōnen (BO3) - LFL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 3 match between TLN Pirates and Esprit Shōnen in the LFL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 6 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "TLN Pirates" if TLN Pirates win the match against Esprit Shōnen.

This market will resolve to "Esprit Shōnen" if Esprit Shōnen win the match against TLN Pirates.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: TLN Pirates, fresh off a dominant 7-2 regular season finish as the No. 2 seed in LFL 2026 Spring, face No. 7 Esprit Shōnen in the upper bracket quarterfinals BO3 on May 6 under fearless draft rules. Pirates capped the round-robin with a 1-0 win over Shōnen on May 1, extending their head-to-head edge amid just two losses all split to teams like Vitality.Bee and ZYB Esport. Shōnen's 3-6 record reflects inconsistency despite a stable roster featuring xKenzuke mid and Mxe support, while Pirates' core of Spooder top, Stefan jungle, and Toffe mid drives trader consensus toward their seeding advantage in this double-elimination playoff path to EMEA Masters qualification. markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 78-79°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ga/atlanta/KATL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a highest temperature of 76-77°F in Atlanta on May 4, backed by real-time observations at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (KATL) station, the market's resolution source via Weather Underground, where midday peaks reached this range under mostly cloudy skies that capped solar insolation. National Weather Service forecasts initially projected highs near 78°F—aligning with the climatological normal—but a surface high pressure ridge ushered unseasonably cool air, 5-10°F below average, with light southwest winds and increasing cloud cover preventing further warming as daylight wanes. Realistic challenges include unexpected clearing for late-afternoon heating, though model ensembles show low probability, with final KATL data expected soon after midnight.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the AZ-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on August 4, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jay Feely's dominant first-quarter fundraising haul exceeding $740,000—far outpacing Joseph Chaplik's $168,000—has fueled trader consensus positioning him as the clear frontrunner in the AZ-01 Republican primary, set for July 21. High-profile endorsements from President Trump, Speaker Johnson, House Republican leadership, and Rep. Juan Ciscomani bolstered Feely's momentum in late April, amplifying his NFL-honed name recognition in this open, competitive Scottsdale-area district. Despite a mid-April NextGen poll showing Chaplik leading narrowly at 24% amid a fragmented field, bettors prioritize Feely's financial edge and institutional backing to consolidate support ahead of early voting in June, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in primary dynamics.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Miami be between 90-91°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Miami International Airport on May 4 project a high temperature near 81°F under partly sunny conditions with a 50% chance of afternoon showers and thunderstorms, driving near-unanimous trader consensus at 99.6% for the 80-81°F outcome as reported by Weather Underground. Post-frontal cooling from a recent weak cold front has introduced persistent cloud cover and sea breeze convergence, reducing solar insolation and capping peak heating below climatological May averages of 86°F—current mid-morning observations at KMIA confirm upper-70s readings amid overcast skies. Realistic challenges include unexpected clearing before peak heating (2-4 PM EDT), potentially nudging into 82°F per model ensembles, with final NWS updates and hourly data through evening shaping resolution.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 44% implied probability to the peak U.S. unemployment rate (U-3 measure) hitting 5.0% or higher in 2026, reflecting skin-in-the-game caution beyond the Federal Reserve's March Summary of Economic Projections median of 4.4% for Q4 2026 and Philadelphia Fed forecasters' 4.5% estimate. March's BLS Employment Situation reported 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains—exceeding expectations—and a dip to 4.3% unemployment, buoyed by near-60-year low initial jobless claims signaling limited layoff risks, yet broader private-sector contraction and recession fears persist amid erratic early-2026 hiring. Key catalysts include the April jobs report on May 8 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, which could recalibrate rate path expectations tied to labor dynamics.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The open seat in New York's 21st Congressional District, vacated by retiring Republican Elise Stefanik, favors the GOP due to its R+10 partisan lean, driving trader consensus to price Republicans at 58.5% for the November 3 general election winner. Recent polls show Joe Constantino commanding a 27-point lead in the June 23 Republican primary, bolstered by President Trump's April 21 endorsement despite bucking the state party's earlier backing of another contender. Democrats lack a clear frontrunner, with their primary undecided amid limited fundraising and visibility. Upcoming primaries could clarify nominees, but the district's historical incumbency patterns and battleground math tilt toward a Republican hold absent major shifts.

markets.question: Will Ethena reach $1.20 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Binance 1-minute candle for Ethena (ENA/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 15:45 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final “High” price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ENA/USDT “High” prices available at:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ENA_USDT with the chart set to “1m” (one-minute candles) on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ENA/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid commands 53% trader consensus for the Hart Memorial Trophy after securing the Art Ross Trophy with a league-high 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists), surging ahead late to edge Nikita Kucherov (130 points) and Nathan MacKinnon (127 points, Rocket Richard goals leader). Despite Edmonton's .567 points percentage and first-round playoff elimination by Anaheim, McDavid's offensive dominance in all Oilers wins bolsters his MVP case amid voter emphasis on individual production. Kucherov's 32% share reflects Tampa Bay's late Eastern surge before their Montreal upset, while MacKinnon's 17.5% stems from elite two-way play driving Colorado's league-best 108 points and advancing playoff run versus Los Angeles. Macklin Celebrini's rookie impact trails at 2.5%. Finalists loom May 8.

Nominations finalized mid-April—Chong on April 9 after a party primary win, Oh on April 18—have shaped a clear head-to-head matchup focused on housing price controls, deregulation, and conservative voter consolidation. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Jannik Sinner as the clear 2026 Wimbledon favorite at 51% implied probability, bolstered by his 2025 grass-court Grand Slam triumph over Carlos Alcaraz in the final and a dominant 29-2 record this season, including ATP Masters 1000 titles at Indian Wells, Miami, and Monte Carlo where he defeated Alcaraz in the championship match to reclaim world No. 1. Alcaraz, at 18%, remains a strong contender with his Australian Open 2026 career Grand Slam completion and two prior Wimbledon wins, though recent head-to-head losses to Sinner temper expectations. Novak Djokovic's 10.3% reflects his seven Wimbledon titles and grass pedigree, but ongoing shoulder and forearm injuries sidelining him from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid have eroded confidence amid his No. 4 ranking. With grass tune-ups still ahead, early clay mastery signals Sinner's all-surface prowess driving the market.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPY) hit (HIGH) $780 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPY) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the S&P 500 (SPY) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 trades near all-time highs around 7,240 as of May 4, 2026—mirroring SPY at roughly $720—following April's 10.4% rally fueled by strong Q1 earnings beats from Magnificent Seven leaders like Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta Platforms reported last week. This momentum reflects trader consensus on resilient corporate revenue growth amid cooling inflation pressures and the Federal Reserve's steady federal funds rate of 3.5%-3.75% after April's unchanged policy decision. Upcoming catalysts include April CPI data on May 12, ADP employment on May 6, nonfarm payrolls, and continued earnings season, which could sway rate cut odds, Treasury yields, and end-of-May index positioning amid elevated valuations.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 dated May 2, 2026, securing her spot among eight artists who've topped the chart this year—including Taylor Swift (two No. 1s: "The Fate of Ophelia" and "Opalite"), Ella Langley's record eight-week dominator "Choosin' Texas," Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, and Mariah Carey. Country and pop crossovers have fueled much of the action amid strong streaming surges and radio play, with Ella poised for a ninth week atop the May 9 chart. Traders should watch weekly updates, May 1 drops like Lady Gaga's "Shape of a Woman" and Maroon 5's "Heroine" for viral potential, and summer album campaigns from Drake or Ariana Grande that could yield late-year No. 1s in a competitive landscape.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bucheon FC 1995, newly promoted to K League 1 after a strong 2025 K League 2 campaign, sit 9th with 6 points from 6 matches (1W-3D-2L) heading into their May 5 home clash against struggling Jeju United at Bucheon Stadium, seeking revenge following a narrow 1-0 away loss on April 4 that capped a four-game winless streak (0W-3D-1L). Jeju, hovering 11th or 12th with 1 win, 2 draws, and 3 losses, gained momentum from that victory but remain in relegation contention amid poor overall form. Head-to-head history favors Jeju (4 wins to Bucheon's 1), though Bucheon's solid draws versus Pohang, Gangwon, Ulsan, and Daejeon highlight resilience; watch for returns from injured winger Seung-bin Kim and midfielder Jong-woo Kim, plus home advantage in this early table battle.

markets.question: Kovacevic vs. Fearnley: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aleksandar Kovacevic and Jacob Fearnley in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification: Aleksandar Kovacevic vs Jacob Fearnley Match O/U 22.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification: Aleksandar Kovacevic vs Jacob Fearnley
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Aleksandar Kovacevic and Jacob Fearnley in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, Qualification, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Aleksandar Kovacevic' if Aleksandar Kovacevic advances against Jacob Fearnley.

This market will resolve to 'Jacob Fearnley' if Jacob Fearnley advances against Aleksandar Kovacevic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Jacob Fearnley as a strong 72.5% favorite over Aleksandar Kovacevic in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia qualifiers, driven primarily by Fearnley's superior clay court record of 11-6 lifetime compared to Kovacevic's dismal 24-34 mark and 3-13 at ATP level. Both players hover around No. 100 in ATP rankings with no head-to-head history, but Kovacevic's recent clay struggles—losses in Cagliari Challenger last week to Marcos Giron, Houston round of 32, and Madrid qualifying—underscore his surface vulnerabilities. Fearnley, despite a rib injury withdrawal from Madrid and mixed 5-10 YTD hard-court form, boasts a 65% clay win rate that tilts the matchup on Rome's red clay.

markets.question: Will Eisenkot join the Bennett-Lapid alliance by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions is met by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The Yashar party ("Yashar! With Eisenkot") or the Together Party announce that the Yashar party will merge with, or contest the 2026 Israeli legislative election on a joint candidate list with, the Together party.

2) Gadi Eisenkot or the Together party announce that Gadi Eisenkot will run on the Together party’s candidate list for the 2026 Israeli legislative election.

Only definitive announcements will qualify. Suggestions, negotiations, offers, invitations, or other non-definitive statements will not count.

If the Together party undergoes a merger, name change, or other restructuring, the resulting party or alliance will be considered the Together party for purposes of this market, provided both Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are members of the resulting party or alliance.

If the Yashar party undergoes a merger, name change, or other restructuring, the resulting party, alliance, or candidate list will be considered the Yashar party for purposes of this market, provided Gadi Eisenkot is a member of the resulting party, alliance, or candidate list.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Yashar party, the Together party, Gadi Eisenkot, Naftali Bennett, and Yair Lapid; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 340-359 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

events.title: NY-13 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party at 93.5% to retain New York's 13th congressional district, driven by its D+32 Cook Partisan Voting Index, Rep. Adriano Espaillat's 83% landslide in the 2024 general election over Republican Ruben Vargas, and consistent 90%+ Democratic margins reflecting massive voter registration advantages in urban Upper Manhattan and Bronx neighborhoods. Recent focus centers on the contested June 23 closed Democratic primary, where Espaillat faces nine challengers including activist Darializa Avila Chevalier—highlighted by her April 20 internal poll showing the incumbent's soft support and April 26 ballot challenges—while Republicans pit Emmanuel Williams against Manual Williams. GOP viability remains remote absent extraordinary developments like a post-primary Democratic scandal, nominee health issues, or seismic national midterm turnout shifts before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will "Miraculous World: Paris, Tales of Shadybug and Claw Noir" be the #2 global Netflix movie this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV movies list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #2 global Netflix movie.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for Global Top 10 Movies (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 8, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Swapped's explosive streaming debut on May 1 has propelled it to the #2 spot on Netflix's global movies chart, trailing only the holdover action hit Apex, with FlixPatrol data confirming its runner-up position amid 17.5 million weekly views for challengers like 180. Michael B. Jordan's voice starring role in this Skydance Animation family body-swap comedy, coupled with strong critical praise for its visual charm and replay value, has fueled broad family audience engagement just days into release. Trader consensus at 97.7% implied probability reflects this momentum, backed by skin-in-the-game bets, though a realistic upset could stem from an unexpected late-week surge in views for recent releases like Roommates or holdovers if Swapped's trajectory softens before charts lock.

markets.question: Spread: Cádiz CF (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Cádiz CF" if Cádiz CF win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "RC Deportivo La Coruña".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Cádiz CF vs. RC Deportivo La Coruña - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Deportivo La Coruña, entrenched in second place in LaLiga Hypermotion standings with 64 points from a strong run of wins and draws—including a 3-1 victory over Mirandés last weekend and a 1-1 draw at Burgos on April 25—enter Jornada 39 as promotion contenders against relegation-battling Cádiz CF, mired in 18th on 38 points. Cádiz's winless streak over five matches features heavy losses like 3-0 to Sporting Gijón and 3-0 at Real Valladolid, exacerbated by injuries to key attackers Javi Ontiveros (groin), Suso (muscle), and Bojan Kovacevic (knee). The earlier season 2-2 draw at Riazor highlights Cádiz's home fightback potential, but Deportivo's away form and table position drive trader sentiment toward the visitors.

markets.question: Will Palantir (PLTR) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Palantir is estimated to release earnings on May 4, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Palantir’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.28 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Palantir reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.28 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Palantir releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 100% implied probability for Palantir (PLTR) beating Q1 2026 non-GAAP EPS consensus of $0.28, driven by the company's after-market earnings release confirming adjusted EPS of $0.33 and revenue of $1.63 billion versus $1.54 billion estimates—a wide-margin beat fueled by 85% year-over-year top-line growth, U.S. commercial revenue surging over 100% on AI Platform (AIP) momentum, and raised FY2026 revenue guidance to $7.65 billion crushing $7.28 billion forecasts. This extends PLTR's streak of earnings beats amid accelerating Rule of 40 scores above 140, reflecting superior competitive positioning in AI software. Tail risks remain minimal but could involve resolution disputes over precise consensus tabulation or auditing of non-GAAP metrics, though the beat's scale minimizes such scenarios.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors AI coding platform Cursor, with implied probabilities near 77%, following SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a major partnership—including a $60 billion acquisition option—to integrate advanced developer tools into xAI's ecosystem amid intensifying AI competition from labs like OpenAI and Anthropic. Caesars Entertainment leads overall at 84% implied odds due to Fertitta Entertainment's March takeover talks valued around $7 billion, while biotech Viking Therapeutics trades at 61% as big pharma targets its VK2735 dual GLP-1/GIP obesity drug candidate. Google's March completion of its $32 billion Wiz cybersecurity acquisition highlights broader tech M&A momentum filling AI gaps, though regulatory scrutiny could delay deals before the December 31, 2026 resolution. Upcoming catalysts include option exercises, bid updates, and phase 3 trial readouts.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' skin-in-the-game consensus prices a tight race for South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation, with 2.7%–2.9% at 42.1% implied probability edging 2.4%–2.6% at 35.0%, reflecting uncertainty over persistent upward pressures versus the Bank of Korea's (BoK) 2% target. March 2026 CPI rose to 2.2% year-over-year—up from 2.0% prior months—amid import price spikes and Middle East tensions fueling energy costs, prompting BoK's April rate hold at 2.5% and fresh hawkish signals on May 4 from the deputy governor eyeing hikes. IMF's April forecast at 2.5% and BoK's revised 2.2% projection underscore mid-to-high 2% risks, with April CPI due May 5 and May 28 policy meeting as key swing catalysts.

markets.question: Will Moonshot have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank among primarily Chinese companies, based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Qualifying Chinese Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Meituan” would be ranked ahead of “Xiaomi”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Baidu leads trader consensus at 45% implied probability after its ERNIE 5.1 Preview release on April 29 topped LMArena's Text Arena leaderboard among Chinese large language models, achieving the highest Elo score of 1476 and securing the top spot in legal, math, and business benchmarks with superior efficiency. This momentum from resolving the prior end-of-April market in Baidu's favor underpins its frontrunner status for the May 31 snapshot on the same leaderboard. Alibaba trails at 19% buoyed by Qwen's broad open-source adoption and recent video-generation leadership, while Zhipu AI (Z.ai) holds 16% on GLM-5.1's coding prowess. ByteDance's Doubao excels in user adoption but lags benchmarks; volatility persists with potential new model drops amid Huawei chip scrambles.

markets.question: Will the fight be won by submission?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, ends by submission, including tapout, verbal submission, or technical submission (referee stoppage due to a submission hold). Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 328: Sean Strickland vs. Khamzat Chimaev (Middleweight, Main Card)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Sean Strickland" if Sean Strickland is officially declared the winner of the fight against Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026.

It will resolve to "Khamzat Chimaev" if Khamzat Chimaev is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: UFC 328's middleweight title main event pits undefeated champion Khamzat Chimaev (15-0) against challenger Sean Strickland (30-7) on May 9 at Prudential Center in Newark, with trader consensus reflecting Chimaev's wrestling pressure versus Strickland's high-volume striking and proven durability in decisions. Fight week tensions peaked via UFC Countdown footage revealing their gym origins—Strickland warning Chimaev against injuring sparring partners—prompting Dana White's mandate for heavy police presence and no face-offs to prevent street altercations. Chimaev returns post-title win over Dricus du Plessis in August 2025, while Strickland rebounds from du Plessis loss with a recent decision over Anthony Hernandez; no injury reports or withdrawals noted ahead of Friday weigh-ins, where weight cuts could sway dynamics given Chimaev's quick finishes (average 8:11 fight time).

markets.question: Will Dawn Buckingham win the 2026 Texas Republican Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton holds a trader consensus edge at 54% implied probability over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn's 42.5% ahead of the May 26 Republican primary runoff, driven by Paxton's stronger performance in the March 3 primary—where he led with 42% to Cornyn's 30%—and recent nonpartisan polls showing him ahead, including a Texas Public Opinion Research survey (April 6-7) with Paxton at 48% to Cornyn's 40% among likely voters, and another indicating an 8-point Paxton lead. Paxton's appeal to the GOP base, bolstered by favorable ratings (+25 net) versus Cornyn's (+3), has fueled the shift, while President Trump's delayed endorsement leaves uncertainty. Escalating attacks over border security highlight the high-stakes contest, with Wesley Hunt primary supporters tilting toward Paxton.

markets.question: Will Katie Porter win the California Governor Election in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to according to the candidate who wins the 2026 California gubernatorial election currently scheduled for November 3, 2026.

If the results of the election are not confirmed by July 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xavier Becerra's surge in recent polls, jumping to 24% in the Gudelunas Strategies survey following Eric Swalwell's April withdrawal and a strong April 22 debate performance, has solidified his edge as trader consensus frontrunner at 40%, reflecting his appeal as experienced former attorney general amid voter priorities like the economy and housing. Tom Steyer trails closely at 35% implied probability, bolstered by heavy self-funding—the most expensive campaign yet—and progressive endorsements including Our Revolution, positioning him as a bold outsider on affordability and climate. The top-two primary on June 2 remains fluid in California's nonpartisan system, with Republicans like Steve Hilton polling near but facing Democratic dominance in the general; mail-in ballots starting soon and further debates could tip the balance for advancement.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at <$1.00 on the final day of trading of the week of May 4 – May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) shares in a narrow $5.00-$6.00 range at 48.5% implied probability, neck-and-neck with >$10 at 47.0%, reflecting high uncertainty ahead of the Q1 2026 earnings livestream on May 7. Current share price hovers near $5.40 amid recent volatility, with analysts forecasting a $0.05 per share loss and 42% revenue decline versus prior year, tempering expectations in a subdued housing market. Bulls price in potential positive guidance on cost efficiencies, inventory positioning, and iBuying volume recovery, contrasting bearish views tied to persistent margin pressures and soft demand; resolution hinges on earnings reaction before the May 8 close, with 52-week highs near $10.87 underscoring breakout potential.

markets.question: WTT - Women's Singles: Canada vs South Korea
markets.description:
This market refers to the table tennis match between Canada and South Korea in a WTT event, scheduled for May 4 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Canada' if Canada wins against South Korea.

This market will resolve to 'Korea' if South Korea wins against Canada.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Canada's win at 50% in this closely contested WTT Women's Singles matchup against South Korea amid the ITTF World Team Table Tennis Championships Finals London 2026, reflecting Korea Republic's uncharacteristic Stage 1a struggles—losses to Chinese Taipei (Shin Yu-bin dropped two matches), Romania, and China—exposing vulnerabilities despite higher ITTF rankings like Kim Na-yeong and Park Gah-yeon. Canada's squad, led by Mo Zhang (WR #57), advanced from Stage 1b Group 7 with 3-0 sweeps over Sri Lanka and Costa Rica before a competitive 2-3 defeat to Sweden, signaling resilience in lower-stakes fixtures. markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices failure risk below 5% through June 30 for listed majors like BNY Mellon (4.7¢ implied probability), Goldman Sachs (1.8¢), and JPMorgan Chase (1¢), reflecting robust Tier 1 capital ratios averaging 13-15% and diversified revenue streams insulating them from sector pressures. Recent FDIC resolutions of two small regionals—Metropolitan Capital Bank ($261 million assets, Jan. 30) and Community Bank West Georgia ($288 million, May 1)—highlight commercial real estate (CRE) distress, with $875 billion in maturities looming, yet big banks hold just 13% CRE exposure versus 44% for regionals. FDIC's April 2026 Risk Review flags credit risks but affirms overall stability; watch June FOMC for rate cut signals easing deposit costs ahead of Q2 earnings.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 32°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Today's (May 4) recorded high of 44°C under persistent dry northwesterly winds and low humidity (8%) exemplifies the high-pressure system suppressing clouds and intensifying pre-monsoon heat, with feels-like temperatures exceeding 50°C during recent alerts. Historical May averages hover near 35°C, but such heatwaves routinely push peaks above 40°C in Sindh plains. Model consensus shows mostly clear skies persisting, though minor sea breeze modulation could cap at 37°C (13.5% odds). PMD station data, typically from Jinnah International Airport, will resolve the market post-event.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on April 24, 2026, plans for new legislation granting powers to proscribe state-backed terrorist organizations like Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), with the bill slated for parliamentary introduction in July—after the June 30 deadline. This follows the EU's January 29 designation of the IRGC amid Tehran's crackdown on protests, alongside mounting calls from UK parliamentarians, the London Assembly, and opposition figures for immediate action. However, the government's preference for tailored legal powers over existing proscription mechanisms, combined with parliamentary timelines, leads traders to price an 83.5% "No" probability, reflecting consensus on procedural delays despite heightened diplomatic pressure from allies.
